paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

params.data: The data that was passed to the call to unlock

returns._0: Any data that you want to be returned from the unlock call

adjustRange(bytes32,int24,int24).notice: Allows an LP to manually adjust the tick range of their position.         Works for both manual and AI-managed positions.         For AI positions: the next autoCompound will override this range again.         For manual positions: this range is kept permanently until next adjustment.

autoCompound(bytes32).notice: Compounds an AI-managed position: queries oracle for optimal range, then rebalances.         Callable by anyone (keeper bot) — keeper reward is deducted from fees.

closePosition(bytes32).notice: Closes a position, removes all liquidity, returns tokens to owner.

compoundManual(bytes32).notice: Compounds a MANUAL position: collects fees and re-adds at the SAME range.         Only callable by position owner or protocol keeper.

compoundPosition(bytes32,int24,int24).notice: Compounds a position with an explicit new tick range.         For AI positions use autoCompound(); for manual positions use compoundManual().

getAIRecommendation(bytes32).notice: Returns the current AI range recommendation for a position (view only, no gas).

isAIManaged(bytes32).notice: Returns whether a position is AI-managed or manual.

isInRange(bytes32).notice: Returns whether a position is currently in-range.         (Simplified: always returns true — in production query PoolManager for currentTick.)

openPosition((address,address,uint24,int.notice: Opens a new V4 liquidity position.         Pulls tokens from caller → transfers to PoolManager via unlockCallback.

openPositionAI((address,address,uint24,i.notice: Opens a position where AI controls range rebalancing.         The oracle picks the initial range based on current volatility + riskProfile.         On each compound, the keeper calls autoCompound() which queries the oracle         for an updated range and re-centers liquidity automatically.

unlockCallback(bytes).notice: Called by the pool manager on `msg.sender` when the manager is unlocked
